title: Can't run containers on Windows 11 / WSL 2
body:
### Issue Description

On a freshly installed podman desktop on Windows 11 with WSL 2, each time I try to start a container from a pulled image I get the following error : 
`Error: (HTTP code 500) server error - crun: cgroups in hybrid mode not supported, drop all controllers from cgroupv2: OCI runtime error`


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install podman desktop on Windows 11 with WSL 2 running
2. Open a podman console
3. Start a container with a simple image : `podman run --rm docker.io/library/hello-world`


### Describe the results you received

```
Trying to pull docker.io/library/hello-world:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 719385e32844 done
Copying config 9c7a54a9a4 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Error: OCI runtime error: crun: cgroups in hybrid mode not supported, drop all controllers from cgroupv2
```

### Describe the results you expected

Expected a message saying the container is running

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

I've tried with several different images, with the same result

comments.body: what is the output of `cat /proc/self/mountinfo` and `cat /sys/fs/cgroup/unified/cgroup.controllers`?

the fedora image has cgroup v1 instead of cgroup v2. 

![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/6017066/cc4f7f92-3da4-4be0-b819-266257894b05)

https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/architecture/cgroups/#check-cgroup-version
do you have any controllers enabled on cgroup v2?  What is the output of `cat /sys/fs/cgroup/unified/cgroup.controllers` and `cat cat /proc/self/mountinfo`?

@giuseppe I am seeing this same issue.

```
[user@kraken ~]$ cat /proc/self/mountinfo | grep -i cgroup
143 142 0:60 / /sys/fs/cgroup ro,nosuid,nodev,noexec shared:12 - tmpfs tmpfs ro,size=4096k,nr_inodes=1024,mode=755
144 143 0:21 / /sys/fs/cgroup/unified rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime shared:13 - cgroup2 cgroup2 rw,nsdelegate
163 143 0:69 / /sys/fs/cgroup/systemd rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime shared:14 - cgroup cgroup rw,xattr,name=systemd
```
 I am not sure how to drop the controller from the v2 group. I would have that that v2 would be preferred over v1? 🤔

```
 akulbe@kraken  ~  podman run quay.io/podman/hello
Error: preparing container 805fd9ff4cbdbf60c3e3cc21a91a1a537420956f099ff511e2b16a37f88aec28 for attach: crun: cgroups in hybrid mode not supported, drop all controllers from cgroupv2: OCI runtime error
```
not sure how that got created, but it is not a configuration we want to support.  The safest bet is probably to go cgroupv2 only since long term anyway cgroupv1 support is going to be removed

@giuseppe I think it may have been this:

```
[wsl2]
memory=64GB

kernelCommandLine=systemd.unified_cgroup_hierarchy=1 cgroup_no_v1=all
```

In my `~\.wslconfig` file. Once I commented that last line out, it worked.

yes, that disables all the controllers on cgroup v1 but still doesn't force the unified mount.

Thanks for letting us know!
